channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning of hackers actively exploiting flaws in Ubiquity UniFi OS and Lantronix serial-to-ethernet servers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Amadey, StealC malware operations disrupted in Operation Endgame action
item.description: Microsoft, Europol, and international partners have disrupted infrastructure used by the Amadey and StealC malware operations as part of Operation Endgame, which targets cybercriminal services and ran...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Securing the service desk: Why social engineering attacks keep succeeding
item.description: Service desks have become a favored target for attackers seeking password resets, MFA changes, and access to corporate accounts. Specops Software breaks down how service desk social engineering attack...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains.
The "critical exploitable pattern" ha...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
item.description: Attackers can exploit the issue to disable security and integrated browser tools without needing administrator privileges or kernel exploits....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
item.description:
We are standing at the end of an era we never thought to mourn: the era of human-speed threats.
For years, cybersecurity moved to a rhythm organizations could follow. A researcher found a bug, a CVE ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stealthy Mistic backdoor linked to ransomware access broker KongTuke
item.description: A new backdoor dubbed Mistic has been observed in financially motivated attacks targeting organizations in the insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DoJ Seizes Huione Cloud Account Tied to Cyber Scam Money Laundering
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Tuesday announced the seizure of a cloud computing account put to use by subsidiaries of Cambodia-based corporate conglomerate HuiOne Group, as the Treasury unv...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root
item.description: Threat actors have begun to exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) and Unified Communications Manager Session Management Editio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
item.description: A high-severity SSRF vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20230, in Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server is now being exploited in attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Tata Electronics confirms cyberattack as hackers leak data
item.description: Tata Electronics has confirmed in a statement to BleepingComputer that it was the target of a cyberattack that impacted parts of its IT infrastructure. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
item.description: More victims have emerged after attackers breached application vendor Klue and used its OAuth tokens to steal customers' Salesforce data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Windows 11 KB5095093 update rolls out new Point-in-Time restore feature
item.description: ​​Microsoft has released the KB5095093 preview cumulative update for Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2, which fixes numerous bugs and begins rolling out new features, including the new Point-in-Time restore fe...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Healthtech firm Xolis suffers data breach impacting 1.4 million people
item.description: Healthcare technology company Xsolis says that sensitive data belonging to nearly 1.4 million individuals was compromised in a phishing attack that gave attackers access to its network. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'Cordyceps': Mushrooming Malicious Pull Requests Threaten Developer Workflows
item.description: The CI/CD workflow weakness affects Microsoft's Azure Sentinel, Google's AI Agent Development Kit, Apache's Doris analytics database, Cloudflare's Workers SDK, and Python Software Foundation's Black....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New macOS ClickFix attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer
item.description: A new macOS ClickFix campaign is using Terminal commands to silently download, mount, and launch info-stealing malware from malicious disk image (DMG) files. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
item.description: A Russian-speaking initial access broker (IAB) driven by financial gain is assessed to be behind a large-scale credential-harvesting operation known as FortiBleed that has targeted over 430,000 FortiG...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport ...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
item.description: Two members of the 'Scattered Spider' cybercrime group pleaded guilty to hacking the Transport for London (TfL) systems in 2024. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake AI Agent Skill Passed Security Scans and Reportedly Reached 26,000 Agents
item.description:
Security firm AIR built a fake AI agent skill, pushed it through a popular skill marketplace and an Instagram ad, and says it reached roughly 26,000 agents, including some on corporate accounts.
Ever...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
item.description:
President Trump signed an executive order on June 22 setting hard deadlines for federal agencies to move high-value assets and high-impact systems to post-quantum cryptography.
Key establishment must...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
item.description: GitHub is moving to strengthen software supply chain security by updating "actions/checkout" to block pwn request attacks that exploit the risky use of the "pull_request_target workflow" trigger to ru...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Exploit Doesn't Exist. You Can Still Prove It Works Against You
item.description: Attackers can now weaponize newly disclosed vulnerabilities far faster than most organizations can patch them. Picus Security explains how security teams can validate exploitability before a public ex...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
item.description: LastPass announced that hackers accessed customer data from its Salesforce environment after stealing the company's OAuth tokens in the Klue supply chain attack earlier this month. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SocGholish Takedown Highlights Malicious TDS Threats
item.description: SocGholish uses traffic distribution systems (TDSs) to provide initial access into victims' networks for cybercrime groups such as the notorious Evil Corp....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
item.description: The threat actors engineered a Golang-based sniffer to target 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and identify 110 million credentials in the ongoing global campaign....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Webinar: Why email security teams are drowning in alerts
item.description: Phishing, BEC, and account takeover attacks continue to overwhelm security teams with alerts and investigations. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help automate detection and response workfl...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Agentic AI: The Weapon That No Longer Needs a Warrior
item.description: Every weapon begins as an extension of the hand that holds it. The spear lengthened the reach of the arm. The bow sent the point flying without the throw. The rifle placed a man's death a quarter mile...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious npm packages that are designed to deliver a Windows-based remote access trojan (RAT).
The list of identified packages, is below -
aes-...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Uses Fake Documents to Install ManageEngine RMM Tool
item.description: Direct messages sent via WhatsApp are being used to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBScript) files that lead to the installation of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) softwar...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
